description_long: This object is a primitive knife featuring a dark, chipped stone blade and a handle made from a textured, light brown material resembling antler or bone. The blade is broad, leaf-shaped, and tapers to a sharp point, with visible flaking patterns typical of knapped stone tools. The handle is cylindrical, slightly flared at the end, and shows natural surface irregularities, suggesting it is crafted from a single piece of antler or bone. The blade and handle are joined by a binding of what appears to be sinew or rawhide, tightly wrapped around the junction for stability. There is no visible guard between the blade and handle, and the overall construction is utilitarian, with no decorative elements. The knife has an ancient, handmade appearance, suitable for display as a historical or survival-themed prop.
description: A primitive knife with a chipped stone blade and a textured antler or bone handle, bound together with sinew. The design is utilitarian and lacks ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the dark stone blade and the textured, light brown handle with sinew binding.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow profile of the blade and the irregular, natural end of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's flaked surface and the cylindrical handle's grain and color.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the flared, rough end of the handle and the thin edge of the blade.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, flared end, made of antler or bone, natural texture, sinew binding
